hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/Y/F) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 residues (e.g., E192, E193, Y284), while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve steric fit in the SSTR2-unique TM6 pocket and reduce off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 binding.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are strong in binding energy and structural quality but lack sufficient selectivity for SSTR2 over other SSTR subtypes. The next iteration will focus on disrupting off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions to improve selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve SSTR2 selectivity, we must disrupt conserved interactions at non-pharmacophore positions (1, 2, 5, 6, 11, 12) that are likely responsible for off-target binding to SSTR3/SSTR5.

These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 without compromising SSTR2 binding. For example, introducing charged residues at positions 1 and 2 can disrupt electrostatic interactions in off-targets.
